explanations.9: The APP_HOME variable is set to /opt/docusaurus if it's not already defined in the environment; the ${VAR:-default} syntax means 'use VAR if it exists, otherwise use the default value after the colon'.
explanations.12: This tells Docker which user account to run the container as, using the same OS_USERID and OS_GROUPID variables from the build args.
explanations.16: The double dot (..) means 'the parent directory of the current directory', so this mounts everything one level up into the container at APP_HOME.
explanations.19: The sleep infinity command keeps the container running indefinitely instead of starting the normal application, which is useful for development where you might run commands manually inside the container.
explanations.25: This is setting a Java environment variable that tells the LanguageTool service where to find its language model files, which it will load from the ngrams volume.
explanations.32: These named volumes are persistent storage areas managed by Docker itself rather than directories on your computer—they survive even if you delete the containers.
